V-M-057.checks: Admin analytics endpoints expose useful MTProto statistics with strict redaction, pagination, filtering, admin-only auth, and audit safety.
V-M-057.commands: pass-local 2026-05-20: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_analytics_api.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_mtproto_ip_observability.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py -v
V-M-057.evidence: Admin analytics API tests pass for Phase-43 summary, timeseries, user/proxy search, explicit raw-IP investigation route, resource metrics, storage budget, alert list/actions, redaction, and non-admin rejection.

verification-levels.module-local: Use FastAPI tests with admin and non-admin clients to verify auth, filters, pagination, time clamping, redaction guards, and promotion tag delegation.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-056 and V-M-059 to prove API schemas match analytics service outputs and promotion tag state.
verification-levels.phase-level: Admin UI and release gates cannot close unless every API response passes secret-redaction assertions.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Phase-43 requires admin-only search/detail, alerts, timeseries, resource metrics, storage budget, and explicit IP-investigation APIs with scoped raw IP exposure.

phase-43-checks.check-1: User/proxy search returns paginated assignments by email, user id, display name, SNI, and status.
phase-43-checks.check-2: User detail returns issued_at, last_seen_at, sessions, traffic, duration, errors, current IPs, last IP, and all IP observations for 90 days.
phase-43-checks.check-3: Timeseries endpoint clamps bucket size/range and returns graph-ready buckets for the selected metric.
phase-43-checks.check-4: Alerts endpoints list, acknowledge, resolve, and expose open high/critical counts without automatic enforcement.
phase-43-checks.check-5: Resource metrics endpoint exposes proxy CPU/RAM/uptime/dropped telemetry state without host secrets.
phase-43-checks.check-6: Explicit IP endpoints are admin-only and audited; general analytics endpoints still reject raw IP values.

module-checks.check-1: Summary endpoint returns total issued, active/disabled/reissue_required counts, active connections, runtime health, availability proof, and traffic windows.
module-checks.check-2: Per-proxy endpoint returns only selected assignment usage, sessions, last seen, errors, abuse signals, and safe owner references.
module-checks.check-3: Event list endpoint enforces pagination, time-window clamping, allowed filters, and stable ordering.
module-checks.check-4: Top-users endpoint supports traffic, duration, connection count, and error count orderings.
module-checks.check-5: Abuse endpoint returns observe-only signals and never changes assignment status.
module-checks.check-6: Promotion tag endpoints delegate validation/state to M-059 and require explicit admin confirmation for mutation.
module-checks.check-7: Non-admin users receive 401 or 403 and no analytics data.
module-checks.check-8: Payload redaction guard rejects full tg links, secrets, raw IPs, runtime tokens, private keys, and full promotion tags.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin summary endpoint returns total issued, active/disabled/reissue counts, active connections, traffic windows, and runtime health.
scenarios.scenario-2: Per-proxy endpoint returns only the selected assignment's safe usage and error data.
scenarios.scenario-3: Events and top-users endpoints enforce pagination, time-window clamping, and admin-only auth.
scenarios.scenario-4: Payload redaction guard rejects full tg links, secrets, raw IPs, runtime tokens, and full promotion tags.
scenarios.scenario-5: Promotion tag update writes audit evidence without logging the full tag.
scenarios.scenario-6: Phase-43 admin user detail exposes raw IP only through the explicit investigation route and never through summary/top-user/general analytics responses.
scenarios.scenario-7: Alert acknowledge/resolve and proxy disable/IP block routes require confirmation and audit-safe details.

redaction-rules.rule-1: General admin analytics APIs may expose masked SNI, masked promotion tag, safe ids, counts, timestamps, status codes, and aggregate bytes only.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Full proxy links, fake-TLS secrets, runtime tokens, private keys, and full promotion tags fail the response guard everywhere.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Raw IP values are allowed only in explicit Phase-43 admin-only investigation responses backed by M-061 and must be absent from logs, audit details, top users, summaries, and general events.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Backend API tests may run with fixture users and in-memory or disposable databases.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Running analytics queries against production requires operator approval if raw event volume is unknown.
